title: Any account can frontrun proxy initialization
reason: This is a generic deployment-time race on an uninitialized proxy, not a demonstrated runtime protocol bug in the reviewed codebase; no non-atomic deployment flow was evidenced.

title: No Access Control on rescueTokens Function
reason: False positive. `Pool.rescueTokens` is protected by `onlyPoolAdmin` before it reaches `PoolLogic.executeRescueTokens`.

title: Unchecked Liquidation Bonus Allows Protocol Loss
reason: This depends on privileged governance writing nonsensical reserve parameters; no permissionless exploit path was shown.

title: No Health Factor Validation When Switching eMode from Category 0
reason: The issue is user-self-inflicted and does not by itself create a protocol-level exploit or bad-debt primitive.

title: Oracle Price Manipulation Risk
reason: Generic oracle-trust concern only. The reviewed code does not show that the configured oracle source is manipulable or attacker-controlled.

title: feeToVault Can Be Set to Zero Address
reason: Not a distinct vulnerability here. The retained vault-fee finding already shows that the vault is never paid at all, regardless of recipient address.

title: No Slippage Protection in Withdraw
reason: Withdrawals are deterministic lending redemptions, not market-priced swaps, so swap-style slippage protection is not applicable.

title: Unbacked Minting Without Health Factor Check
reason: `mintUnbacked` is restricted to the trusted bridge role, and the resulting exposure is explicitly tracked via `reserve.unbacked`; this is not a permissionless exploit.

title: FlashLoan Premium Can Be Set to 100%
reason: High premiums alone are a governance choice, not a vulnerability. The retained flashloan finding covers the concrete missing split invariant that can break repayment.

title: Rebalance Stable Rate Does Not Validate New Rate
reason: Stable-rate rebalancing is intended protocol behavior and is gated by `ValidationLogic.validateRebalanceStableBorrowRate`.

title: setLendingGauge Allows Zero Address Without Validation
reason: A zero gauge is handled by the surrounding code paths and merely disables reward-allocation updates; no security impact was shown.

title: Missing Validation for Liquidation Bonus vs LTV Relationship
reason: This is another privileged configuration-hygiene concern without a concrete exploit path beyond governance setting arbitrary bad parameters.

title: Division by Zero in Health Factor Calculation
reason: False positive. The zero-collateral case is explicitly handled, and the shown code path does not divide by zero.

title: No Access Control on initialize Function After First Initialization
reason: False positive. `initializer` prevents repeated calls, and front-running `Pool.initialize` does not seize privileges or change the immutable provider.

title: Integer Overflow in Isolation Mode Debt Calculation
reason: False positive. Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic plus `SafeCast` and bounded debt-ceiling storage prevent silent truncation/overflow here.

title: Missing Validation for Debt Ceiling in Isolation Mode
reason: A large debt ceiling chosen by governance is a risk-parameter decision, not a code vulnerability by itself.
